mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, which boosts corporate profit expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative or flat returns during easing periods, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings potential, creating a persistent upward pressure on equity prices and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently coincide with positive equity returns, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates a soft landing environment, which maximizes risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling FEDFUNDS does not outperform baseline returns, or the interaction effect is negative in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal income during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening consumer purchasing power, which erodes corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Falling RSAFS coincides with positive equity returns in late-cycle periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.
